  ---
title: "sampling_type_analysis_04_06_2026"
format: html
---
###Load Libraries

```{r load libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(rstatix)
library(patchwork)
library(rstatix)
library(multcompView)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(janitor)
library(stringr)
library(dplyr)
library(leaflet)
library(sf)
library(patchwork)
library(purrr)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(dbscan)
library(broom)
library(scales)
library(car)
library(kableExtra)
library(e1071)
library(corrplot)
library(tidyr)
library(ggrepel)
library(multcompView)
library(rstatix)
library(sf)
library(sp)
library(gstat)
library(spdep)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(FNN)

```


### Load + Clean Data:

```{r data pull, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# -----------------------------
# Load + clean to analysis-ready
# -----------------------------

nutrients <- c("lbc_1","lb_ceq","p_h_2","ca","k","mg","mn","p","zn")

lime_clean <- read_csv("../data_do_not_push/2021 Liming Study Soil Data Compiled (lbs. A).csv") %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  separate(sample, into = c("type", "sample_site"), sep = ",\\s*", remove = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    # standardize site label
    sample_site = str_squish(sample_site),
    sample_site = str_to_lower(sample_site),
    sample_site = str_replace_all(sample_site, "\\s+", "_"),

    # standardize type labels
    type = str_squish(type),

    # classify sampling category + depth
    sampling_class = case_when(
      type %in% c("A","B") ~ "SP",
      type %in% c("0-4","4-8") ~ "C",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    depth = case_when(
      type == "0-4" ~ "0-4",
      type == "4-8" ~ "4-8",
      type %in% c("A","B") ~ NA_character_,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # replicate id for SP
    sp_rep = case_when(
      type == "A" ~ "A",
      type == "B" ~ "B",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),

    # composite depth label (useful later)
    comp_depth = case_when(
      sampling_class == "C" ~ depth,
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  # create a stable location id (site + sample site + coords)
  group_by(site, sample_site, lat, long) %>%
  mutate(location_id = cur_group_id()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  # keep only relevant types
  filter(type %in% c("0-4","4-8","A","B")) %>%
  # make key factors ordered
  mutate(
    sampling_class = factor(sampling_class, levels = c("SP","C")),
    comp_depth = factor(comp_depth, levels = c("0-4","4-8")),
    sp_rep = factor(sp_rep, levels = c("A","B"))
  )

# Long format for plotting / modeling
lime_long <- lime_clean %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(nutrients), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = nutrients))

```




### Exploratory Plots

```{r}
#distribution curves of 0-4 comps vs 4-8 comps vs SP A and B (A and Bs can be together)
nutrients_all <- c("ca","k","mg","mn","p","zn","p_h_2","lbc_1","lb_ceq")
nutrients_to_log <- setdiff(nutrients_all, "p_h_2")

nutrient_names <- c(
  "ca"     = "Calcium (Ca)",
  "k"      = "Potassium (K)",
  "mg"     = "Magnesium (Mg)",
  "mn"     = "Manganese (Mn)",
  "p"      = "Phosphorus (P)",
  "zn"     = "Zinc (Zn)",
  "p_h_2"  = "pH",
  "lbc_1"  = "Lime Buffer Capacity (LBC)",
  "lb_ceq" = "Lime Buffer Capacity Equivalent (LBCeq)"
)

lime_plot <- lime_clean %>%
  # log1p everything except pH (this is the key change)
  mutate(across(all_of(nutrients_to_log), ~ log1p(.))) %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(nutrients_all), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    sampling_type3 = case_when(
      type == "0-4" ~ "Composite 0–10 cm",
      type == "4-8" ~ "Composite 10-20 cm",
      type %in% c("A","B") ~ "Single Point (A+B)",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    sampling_type3 = factor(
      sampling_type3,
      levels = c("Composite 0–10 cm", "Composite 10-20 cm", "Single Point (A+B)")
    ),
    nutrient = dplyr::recode(nutrient, !!!nutrient_names),
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = unname(nutrient_names))
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sampling_type3), !is.na(value))

p_den <- ggplot(lime_plot, aes(x = value, fill = sampling_type3)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Composite 0–10 cm" = "#1f77b4",
      "Composite 10-20 cm" = "#2ca02c",
      "Single Point (A+B)" = "#ff7f0e"
    ),
    na.translate = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Value (log1p-transformed except pH)",
    y = "Density",
    title = "Density Plots of Bermuda Hay Grassland Soil Nutrients by Sampling Type",
    fill = "Sampling Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p_den


ggsave("../output_do_not_push/nutrient_density_plots_by_sampling_type.png",
       p_den, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300, units = "in", limitsize = FALSE)

```

```{r all_standard_checks, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# =========================================================
# ALL-IN-ONE DATA + MODEL CHECKS
# Assumes lime_clean already exists from  cleaning code
# =========================================================

# --- 1. Rebuild long data for checks ---
check_long <- lime_clean %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(nutrients_to_log), ~ log1p(.))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(nutrients_all), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = nutrients_all),
    sampling_type3 = case_when(
      type == "0-4" ~ "Composite 0-10 cm",
      type == "4-8" ~ "Composite 10-20 cm",
      type %in% c("A", "B") ~ "Single Point (A+B)",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    sampling_type3 = factor(
      sampling_type3,
      levels = c("Composite 0-10 cm", "Composite 10-20 cm", "Single Point (A+B)")
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(sampling_type3))

# --- 2. Basic structure checks ---
cat("===== BASIC STRUCTURE =====\n")
glimpse(lime_clean)

cat("\n===== MISSINGNESS BY NUTRIENT =====\n")
missing_summary <- lime_clean %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(nutrients_all), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "n_missing") %>%
  arrange(desc(n_missing))
print(missing_summary)

cat("\n===== GROUP COUNTS =====\n")
group_counts <- lime_clean %>%
  count(site, sample_site, sampling_class, comp_depth, sp_rep)
print(group_counts, n = 50)

cat("\n===== RECORDS PER LOCATION_ID =====\n")
location_counts <- lime_clean %>%
  count(location_id) %>%
  count(n, name = "n_locations")
print(location_counts)

cat("\n===== LOCATIONS WITH UNEXPECTED RECORD COUNTS =====\n")
odd_locations <- lime_clean %>%
  count(location_id) %>%
  filter(n != 4)
print(odd_locations, n = 50)

# --- 3. Distribution summaries by nutrient x sampling group ---
cat("\n===== DISTRIBUTION SUMMARY =====\n")
dist_summary <- check_long %>%
  group_by(nutrient, sampling_type3) %>%
  summarise(
    n = sum(!is.na(value)),
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = IQR(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    min = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    max = max(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    skewness = e1071::skewness(value, na.rm = TRUE, type = 2),
    kurtosis = e1071::kurtosis(value, na.rm = TRUE, type = 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
print(dist_summary, n = 100)

# --- 4. Shapiro normality by nutrient x sampling group ---
cat("\n===== SHAPIRO NORMALITY TESTS =====\n")
normality_results <- check_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(value)) %>%
  group_by(nutrient, sampling_type3) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    shapiro_p = if (n() >= 3 && n() <= 5000) shapiro.test(value)$p.value else NA_real_,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    normality_flag = case_when(
      is.na(shapiro_p) ~ "not tested",
      shapiro_p < 0.05 ~ "non-normal",
      TRUE ~ "approx. normal"
    )
  )
print(normality_results, n = 100)

# --- 5. Homogeneity of variance across sampling groups ---
cat("\n===== LEVENE VARIANCE TESTS =====\n")
variance_results <- check_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(value)) %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    lev <- car::leveneTest(value ~ sampling_type3, data = .x)
    tibble(
      levene_f = lev$`F value`[1],
      levene_p = lev$`Pr(>F)`[1]
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    variance_flag = case_when(
      is.na(levene_p) ~ "not tested",
      levene_p < 0.05 ~ "heterogeneous variance",
      TRUE ~ "roughly homogeneous variance"
    )
  )
print(variance_results, n = 100)

# --- 6. Correlation among nutrients ---
cat("\n===== SPEARMAN CORRELATION MATRIX =====\n")
corr_data <- lime_clean %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(nutrients_to_log), ~ log1p(.))) %>%
  select(all_of(nutrients_all))

corr_mat <- cor(corr_data, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman")
print(round(corr_mat, 2))

corrplot::corrplot(
  corr_mat,
  method = "color",
  type = "upper",
  tl.col = "black",
  tl.cex = 0.8
)

# --- 7. Residual diagnostics from simple fixed-effects linear models ---
# Model form: value ~ sampling_type3
# This is mainly for checking whether residuals look remotely usable
#  can later replace with a fuller model if needed.

cat("\n===== RESIDUAL DIAGNOSTICS: LINEAR MODELS =====\n")

resid_diag <- check_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(value), !is.na(sampling_type3)) %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    dat <- .x
    
    mod <- lm(value ~ sampling_type3, data = dat)
    res <- resid(mod)
    fit <- fitted(mod)
    
    tibble(
      n = nrow(dat),
      resid_shapiro_p = if(length(res) >= 3 && length(res) <= 5000) shapiro.test(res)$p.value else NA_real_,
      resid_mean = mean(res, na.rm = TRUE),
      resid_sd = sd(res, na.rm = TRUE),
      model_r2 = summary(mod)$r.squared
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    resid_normality_flag = case_when(
      is.na(resid_shapiro_p) ~ "not tested",
      resid_shapiro_p < 0.05 ~ "residuals non-normal",
      TRUE ~ "residuals approx. normal"
    )
  )

print(resid_diag, n = 100)

# --- 8. Residual plots for each nutrient ---
# Produces residual vs fitted and QQ plot for each nutrient

resid_plot_data <- check_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(value), !is.na(sampling_type3)) %>%
  group_split(nutrient)

resid_plot_list <- purrr::map(resid_plot_data, function(dat) {
  nutr <- unique(dat$nutrient)
  mod <- lm(value ~ sampling_type3, data = dat)
  
  aug <- broom::augment(mod)
  
  p1 <- ggplot(aug, aes(x = .fitted, y = .resid)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    labs(
      title = paste(nutr, "- Residuals vs Fitted"),
      x = "Fitted values",
      y = "Residuals"
    )
  
  p2 <- ggplot(aug, aes(sample = .std.resid)) +
    stat_qq(alpha = 0.7) +
    stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    labs(
      title = paste(nutr, "- Normal Q-Q"),
      x = "Theoretical quantiles",
      y = "Standardized residuals"
    )
  
  p1 + p2
})

for(i in seq_along(resid_plot_list)) {
  print(resid_plot_list[[i]])
}

# --- 9. Optional mixed model residual checks ---
# Use this only to account for repeated structure by site/sample_site/location_id
# Adjust random effects if this overfits or becomes singular

cat("\n===== OPTIONAL MIXED MODEL RESIDUAL CHECKS =====\n")

mixed_diag <- check_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(value), !is.na(sampling_type3)) %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    dat <- .x
    
    out <- tryCatch({
      mod <- lmer(value ~ sampling_type3 + (1|site) + (1|sample_site), data = dat)
      res <- resid(mod)
      tibble(
        n = nrow(dat),
        singular = lme4::isSingular(mod, tol = 1e-5),
        AIC = AIC(mod),
        resid_shapiro_p = if(length(res) >= 3 && length(res) <= 5000) shapiro.test(res)$p.value else NA_real_
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      tibble(
        n = nrow(dat),
        singular = NA,
        AIC = NA_real_,
        resid_shapiro_p = NA_real_
      )
    })
    
    out
  }) %>%
  ungroup()

print(mixed_diag, n = 100)

# --- 10. Save key tables if wanted ---
write_csv(normality_results, "../output_do_not_push/standard_checks/normality_results.csv")
write_csv(variance_results, "../output_do_not_push/standard_checks/variance_results.csv")
write_csv(resid_diag, "../output_do_not_push/standard_checks/residual_diagnostics_lm.csv")
write_csv(mixed_diag, "../output_do_not_push/standard_checks/residual_diagnostics_mixed.csv")

```


```{r location_level_exploration, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# =========================================================
# LOCATION-LEVEL WIDE DATA + EXPLORATION
# =========================================================

nutrients_all <- c("ca","k","mg","mn","p","zn","p_h_2","lbc_1","lb_ceq")
nutrients_to_log <- setdiff(nutrients_all, "p_h_2")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Create a clean sample label
# ---------------------------------------------------------
lime_tagged <- lime_clean %>%
  mutate(
    sample_type = case_when(
      type == "A"   ~ "SP_A",
      type == "B"   ~ "SP_B",
      type == "0-4" ~ "COMP_0_10",
      type == "4-8" ~ "COMP_10_20",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    sample_type = factor(
      sample_type,
      levels = c("SP_A", "SP_B", "COMP_0_10", "COMP_10_20")
    )
  )

# quick confirmation
lime_tagged %>%
  count(site, sample_type)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Build location-level wide dataset
# one row = one location_id
# ---------------------------------------------------------
lime_wide <- lime_tagged %>%
  select(site, sample_site, location_id, lat, long, sample_type, all_of(nutrients_all)) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = sample_type,
    values_from = all_of(nutrients_all),
    names_glue = "{.value}_{sample_type}"
  ) %>%
  arrange(site, sample_site)

glimpse(lime_wide)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Create SP summaries from A and B
# mean, median, abs diff, signed diff
# ---------------------------------------------------------
for (nut in nutrients_all) {
  a_col <- paste0(nut, "_SP_A")
  b_col <- paste0(nut, "_SP_B")
  
  lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_SP_mean")]]   <- rowMeans(lime_wide[, c(a_col, b_col)], na.rm = TRUE)
  lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_SP_median")]] <- apply(lime_wide[, c(a_col, b_col)], 1, median, na.rm = TRUE)
  lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_SP_absdiff")]] <- abs(lime_wide[[a_col]] - lime_wide[[b_col]])
  lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_SP_diff")]]    <- lime_wide[[a_col]] - lime_wide[[b_col]]
}

# peek
lime_wide %>%
  select(site, sample_site, location_id,
         ca_SP_A, ca_SP_B, ca_SP_mean, ca_COMP_0_10, ca_COMP_10_20) %>%
  print(n = 10)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 4. A vs B agreement summaries
# ---------------------------------------------------------
ab_agreement <- purrr::map_dfr(nutrients_all, function(nut) {
  a <- lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_SP_A")]]
  b <- lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_SP_B")]]
  d <- a - b
  m <- (a + b) / 2
  
  tibble(
    nutrient = nut,
    n = sum(!is.na(a) & !is.na(b)),
    mean_A = mean(a, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_B = mean(b, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_diff = mean(d, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_abs_diff = median(abs(d), na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_abs_diff = mean(abs(d), na.rm = TRUE),
    spearman_rho = cor(a, b, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"),
    pearson_r = cor(a, b, method = "pearson", use = "complete.obs")
  )
})

ab_agreement

# optional save
# write_csv(ab_agreement, "../output_do_not_push/ab_agreement_summary.csv")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Long table for A vs B plotting
# ---------------------------------------------------------
ab_long <- lime_tagged %>%
  filter(sample_type %in% c("SP_A", "SP_B")) %>%
  select(site, sample_site, location_id, sample_type, all_of(nutrients_all)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(nutrients_all), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Site x sample-type exploratory plots
# Use log1p for all except pH
# ---------------------------------------------------------
plot_long <- lime_tagged %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(nutrients_to_log), ~ log1p(.))) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(nutrients_all), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    sample_type = factor(sample_type, levels = c("SP_A", "SP_B", "COMP_0_10", "COMP_10_20"))
  )

# boxplots by site and sample type
p_site_box <- ggplot(plot_long, aes(x = sample_type, y = value, fill = sample_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.75, outlier.alpha = 0.4) +
  facet_grid(nutrient ~ site, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Sample type",
    y = "Value (log1p except pH)",
    title = "Soil nutrient distributions by site and sample type"
  )

p_site_box

# optional
# ggsave("../output_do_not_push/site_sampletype_boxplots.png",
#        p_site_box, width = 14, height = 16, dpi = 300)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 7. SP A vs B scatterplots with 1:1 line
# ---------------------------------------------------------
ab_scatter_data <- purrr::map_dfr(nutrients_all, function(nut) {
  tibble(
    site = lime_wide$site,
    sample_site = lime_wide$sample_site,
    location_id = lime_wide$location_id,
    nutrient = nut,
    A = lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_SP_A")]],
    B = lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_SP_B")]]
  )
})

p_ab_scatter <- ggplot(ab_scatter_data, aes(x = A, y = B, color = site)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Agreement between SP A and SP B",
    x = "SP A",
    y = "SP B"
  )

p_ab_scatter

# optional
# ggsave("../output_do_not_push/SPA_SPB_scatterplots.png",
#        p_ab_scatter, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Bland-Altman style plots for A vs B
# ---------------------------------------------------------
ab_ba_data <- purrr::map_dfr(nutrients_all, function(nut) {
  a <- lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_SP_A")]]
  b <- lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_SP_B")]]
  
  tibble(
    site = lime_wide$site,
    sample_site = lime_wide$sample_site,
    location_id = lime_wide$location_id,
    nutrient = nut,
    mean_ab = (a + b) / 2,
    diff_ab = a - b
  )
})

p_ba <- ggplot(ab_ba_data, aes(x = mean_ab, y = diff_ab, color = site)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Bland-Altman style plots for SP A vs SP B",
    x = "Mean of A and B",
    y = "A - B"
  )

p_ba

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 9. Build location-level repeated table for later tests
# Table for Friedman / paired tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------
repeated_long <- purrr::map_dfr(nutrients_all, function(nut) {
  tibble(
    site = lime_wide$site,
    sample_site = lime_wide$sample_site,
    location_id = lime_wide$location_id,
    nutrient = nut,
    SP_mean = lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_SP_mean")]],
    COMP_0_10 = lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_COMP_0_10")]],
    COMP_10_20 = lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_COMP_10_20")]]
  )
}) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(SP_mean, COMP_0_10, COMP_10_20),
               names_to = "sample_support",
               values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    sample_support = factor(sample_support, levels = c("SP_mean", "COMP_0_10", "COMP_10_20"))
  )

repeated_long %>%
  count(site, nutrient, sample_support)

# optional save
write_csv(lime_wide, "../output_do_not_push/standard_checks/lime_wide_location_level.csv")
write_csv(repeated_long, "../output_do_not_push/standard_checks/repeated_long_for_tests.csv")

```
```{r, eval = FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#saving to export to arcgis pro
sp_a_sf <- lime_clean %>%
  filter(type == "A") %>%
  select(site, sample_site, location_id, lat, long) %>%
  distinct() %>%
  st_as_sf(coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4326)

st_write(
  sp_a_sf,
  "../output_do_not_push/sp_a_points_all_sites.shp",
  delete_layer = TRUE
)

st_write(
  sp_a_sf,
  "../output_do_not_push/sp_a_points_all_sites.gpkg",
  delete_layer = TRUE
)

```

```{r formal_repeated_tests, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}


# repeated_long assumed to already exist
# columns: site, sample_site, location_id, nutrient, sample_support, value
# sample_support levels: SP_mean, COMP_0_10, COMP_10_20

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Friedman tests pooled across all sites
# ---------------------------------------------------------
friedman_all <- repeated_long %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  friedman_test(value ~ sample_support | location_id) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p_adj_BH = p.adjust(p, method = "BH"))

friedman_all

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Post hoc paired Wilcoxon pooled across all sites
# ---------------------------------------------------------
pairwise_all <- repeated_long %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  pairwise_wilcox_test(
    value ~ sample_support,
    paired = TRUE,
    p.adjust.method = "BH"
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

pairwise_all

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Friedman tests within site
# ---------------------------------------------------------
friedman_by_site <- repeated_long %>%
  group_by(site, nutrient) %>%
  friedman_test(value ~ sample_support | location_id) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(site) %>%
  mutate(p_adj_BH = p.adjust(p, method = "BH")) %>%
  ungroup()

friedman_by_site

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Post hoc paired Wilcoxon within site
# ---------------------------------------------------------
pairwise_by_site <- repeated_long %>%
  group_by(site, nutrient) %>%
  pairwise_wilcox_test(
    value ~ sample_support,
    paired = TRUE,
    p.adjust.method = "BH"
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

pairwise_by_site

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Effect-size style summaries to help interpretation
# ---------------------------------------------------------
support_summary <- repeated_long %>%
  group_by(site, nutrient, sample_support) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = IQR(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

support_summary

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 6. SP A vs SP B paired test for local heterogeneity / bias
# ---------------------------------------------------------
ab_long_for_test <- lime_tagged %>%
  filter(sample_type %in% c("SP_A", "SP_B")) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(nutrients_all), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value") %>%
  select(site, sample_site, location_id, nutrient, sample_type, value)

ab_wilcox <- ab_long_for_test %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  pairwise_wilcox_test(
    value ~ sample_type,
    paired = TRUE,
    p.adjust.method = "BH"
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

ab_wilcox

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Optional: site-specific A vs B
# ---------------------------------------------------------
ab_wilcox_by_site <- ab_long_for_test %>%
  group_by(site, nutrient) %>%
  pairwise_wilcox_test(
    value ~ sample_type,
    paired = TRUE,
    p.adjust.method = "BH"
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

ab_wilcox_by_site

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Save outputs if desired
# ---------------------------------------------------------
write_csv(friedman_all, "../output_do_not_push/lime_results/friedman_all.csv")
write_csv(pairwise_all, "../output_do_not_push/lime_results/pairwise_all.csv")
write_csv(friedman_by_site, "../output_do_not_push/lime_results/friedman_by_site.csv")
write_csv(pairwise_by_site, "../output_do_not_push/lime_results/pairwise_by_site.csv")
write_csv(support_summary, "../output_do_not_push/lime_results/support_summary.csv")
write_csv(ab_wilcox, "../output_do_not_push/lime_results/ab_wilcox.csv")
write_csv(ab_wilcox_by_site, "../output_do_not_push/lime_results/ab_wilcox_by_site.csv")


#across all sites, all nutrients differ between SP (0-20) and COMP (0-10), except for pH and K, which are consistently non-significant. 


```
```{r summary_tables_for_writeup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}



# 1) Pooled support summaries
support_summary_all <- repeated_long %>%
  group_by(nutrient, sample_support) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = IQR(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

support_summary_all

# 2) Wide summary table for writing
support_summary_wide <- support_summary_all %>%
  select(nutrient, sample_support, median, IQR) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = sample_support,
    values_from = c(median, IQR)
  )

support_summary_wide

# 3) SP A vs B local variability
ab_variability <- lime_wide %>%
  transmute(
    site, sample_site, location_id,
    across(ends_with("_SP_absdiff"))
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = ends_with("_SP_absdiff"),
    names_to = "nutrient",
    values_to = "abs_diff"
  ) %>%
  mutate(nutrient = str_remove(nutrient, "_SP_absdiff"))

ab_variability_summary <- ab_variability %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median_abs_diff = median(abs_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_abs_diff = mean(abs_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_abs_diff = IQR(abs_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ab_variability_summary

# 4) Same thing by site
ab_variability_by_site <- ab_variability %>%
  group_by(site, nutrient) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median_abs_diff = median(abs_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_abs_diff = mean(abs_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_abs_diff = IQR(abs_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ab_variability_by_site

```

```{r final_results_table, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}



# support_summary_wide assumed to exist
# friedman_all assumed to exist
# pairwise_all assumed to exist

pairwise_labels <- pairwise_all %>%
  mutate(
    contrast = case_when(
      group1 == "SP_mean"   & group2 == "COMP_0_10"  ~ "SP_vs_C0_10",
      group1 == "SP_mean"   & group2 == "COMP_10_20" ~ "SP_vs_C10_20",
      group1 == "COMP_0_10" & group2 == "COMP_10_20" ~ "C0_10_vs_C10_20",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    sig = case_when(
      p.adj <= 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.adj <= 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.adj <= 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ "ns"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(nutrient, contrast, p.adj, sig) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = contrast,
    values_from = c(p.adj, sig)
  )

final_results_table <- support_summary_wide %>%
  left_join(
    friedman_all %>% select(nutrient, friedman_p = p, friedman_p_adj = p_adj_BH),
    by = "nutrient"
  ) %>%
  left_join(pairwise_labels, by = "nutrient") %>%
  mutate(
    pattern = case_when(
      median_COMP_0_10 > median_SP_mean & median_SP_mean > median_COMP_10_20 ~ "C0_10 > SP > C10_20",
      abs(median_COMP_0_10 - median_SP_mean) < 1e-8 & median_SP_mean > median_COMP_10_20 ~ "C0_10 = SP > C10_20",
      median_COMP_10_20 > median_COMP_0_10 & median_COMP_0_10 > median_SP_mean ~ "C10_20 > C0_10 > SP",
      median_COMP_10_20 > median_SP_mean & median_SP_mean >= median_COMP_0_10 ~ "C10_20 > SP >= C0_10",
      median_COMP_10_20 > median_COMP_0_10 & abs(median_COMP_0_10 - median_SP_mean) < 1e-8 ~ "C10_20 > C0_10 = SP",
      TRUE ~ "mixed"
    )
  )

final_results_table

```

```{r spatial_all_nutrients_run, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# -----------------------------
# 1. Spatial setup
# -----------------------------
spatial_dat <- lime_wide %>%
  select(
    site, sample_site, location_id, lat, long,
    ends_with("_SP_mean"),
    ends_with("_COMP_0_10"),
    ends_with("_COMP_10_20")
  )

spatial_sf <- st_as_sf(
  spatial_dat,
  coords = c("long", "lat"),
  crs = 4326
)

# Georgia: UTM 17N
spatial_sf_utm <- st_transform(spatial_sf, 32617)

# -----------------------------
# 2. Variables to run
# -----------------------------
nutrients_all <- c("ca","k","lb_ceq","lbc_1","mg","mn","p","p_h_2","zn")
supports_all  <- c("SP_mean","COMP_0_10","COMP_10_20")
sites <- c("ROME", "Watkinsville", "Midville", "Tifton")

vars_all <- expand.grid(
  nutrient = nutrients_all,
  support = supports_all,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  mutate(variable = paste0(nutrient, "_", support)) %>%
  pull(variable)

# -----------------------------
# 3. Moran's I function
# -----------------------------
run_morans_i <- function(data_sf, value_col, site_name, k = 5) {
  
  dat_site <- data_sf %>%
    filter(site == site_name) %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[value_col]]))
  
  coords <- st_coordinates(dat_site)
  
  nb <- knearneigh(coords, k = k) %>%
    knn2nb()
  
  lw <- nb2listw(nb, style = "W")
  mt <- moran.test(dat_site[[value_col]], lw)
  
  tibble(
    site = site_name,
    variable = value_col,
    n = nrow(dat_site),
    k = k,
    moran_I = unname(mt$estimate[1]),
    expectation = unname(mt$estimate[2]),
    variance = unname(mt$estimate[3]),
    p_value = mt$p.value
  )
}

# -----------------------------
# 4. Run Moran's I for all
# -----------------------------
morans_results_all <- purrr::map_dfr(
  vars_all,
  ~ purrr::map_dfr(sites, \(s) run_morans_i(spatial_sf_utm, .x, s, k = 5))
) %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = str_remove(variable, "_(SP_mean|COMP_0_10|COMP_10_20)$"),
    support = str_extract(variable, "(SP_mean|COMP_0_10|COMP_10_20)$"),
    p_adj_BH = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH"),
    sig = case_when(
      p_adj_BH <= 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_adj_BH <= 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_adj_BH <= 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ "ns"
    ),
    moran_takeaway = case_when(
      p_adj_BH <= 0.05 & moran_I > 0 ~ "positive spatial autocorrelation",
      p_adj_BH <= 0.05 & moran_I < 0 ~ "negative spatial autocorrelation",
      TRUE ~ "no clear spatial autocorrelation"
    )
  ) %>%
  select(site, nutrient, support, n, k, moran_I, p_value, p_adj_BH, sig, moran_takeaway, variable)

morans_results_all

# -----------------------------
# 5. Empirical variogram function
# -----------------------------
run_variogram <- function(data_sf, value_col, site_name, cutoff = NULL, width = NULL) {
  
  dat_site <- data_sf %>%
    filter(site == site_name) %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[value_col]]))
  
  dat_sp <- as(dat_site, "Spatial")
  coords <- coordinates(dat_sp)
  
  if (is.null(cutoff)) {
    dmax <- max(dist(coords))
    cutoff <- dmax / 2
  }
  
  if (is.null(width)) {
    width <- cutoff / 10
  }
  
  form <- as.formula(paste(value_col, "~ 1"))
  v <- gstat::variogram(form, data = dat_sp, cutoff = cutoff, width = width)
  
  as_tibble(v) %>%
    mutate(
      site = site_name,
      variable = value_col
    )
}

# -----------------------------
# 6. Run empirical variograms for all
# -----------------------------
empirical_variograms_all <- purrr::map_dfr(
  vars_all,
  ~ purrr::map_dfr(sites, \(s) run_variogram(spatial_sf_utm, .x, s))
) %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = str_remove(variable, "_(SP_mean|COMP_0_10|COMP_10_20)$"),
    support = str_extract(variable, "(SP_mean|COMP_0_10|COMP_10_20)$")
  ) %>%
  select(site, nutrient, support, variable, everything())

empirical_variograms_all

# -----------------------------
# 7. Simple semivariogram summary table
#    This is the key table to send me
# -----------------------------
variogram_summary_all <- empirical_variograms_all %>%
  group_by(site, nutrient, support, variable) %>%
  summarise(
    n_lags = n(),
    first_dist = first(dist),
    last_dist = last(dist),
    first_gamma = first(gamma),
    last_gamma = last(gamma),
    max_gamma = max(gamma, na.rm = TRUE),
    lag_at_max_gamma = dist[which.max(gamma)],
    mean_gamma = mean(gamma, na.rm = TRUE),
    spearman_dist_gamma = suppressWarnings(cor(dist, gamma, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")),
    ratio_last_first = ifelse(first(gamma) == 0, NA_real_, last(gamma) / first(gamma)),
    semivar_takeaway = case_when(
      is.na(spearman_dist_gamma) ~ "unclear",
      spearman_dist_gamma >= 0.5 ~ "clear increasing semivariance",
      spearman_dist_gamma >= 0.2 ~ "weak-moderate increasing semivariance",
      spearman_dist_gamma > -0.2 ~ "flat or unclear semivariance pattern",
      TRUE ~ "decreasing/irregular pattern"
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

variogram_summary_all

# -----------------------------
# 8. Save the three tables
# -----------------------------
write_csv(morans_results_all, "../output_do_not_push/standard_checks/morans_I_results_all.csv")
write_csv(empirical_variograms_all, "../output_do_not_push/standard_checks/empirical_variograms_all.csv")
write_csv(variogram_summary_all, "../output_do_not_push/standard_checks/variogram_summary_all.csv")
#spatial autocorrelation found in P, Ca, but not in Watkinsville. Spatial structure is site specific. 
nutrient_labs <- c(
  ca     = "Calcium (Ca)",
  k      = "Potassium (K)",
  lb_ceq = "Lime Buffer Capacity (Equivalent)",
  lbc_1  = "Lime Buffer Capacity",
  mg     = "Magnesium (Mg)",
  mn     = "Manganese (Mn)",
  p      = "Phosphorus (P)",
  p_h_2  = "pH",
  zn     = "Zinc (Zn)"
)

support_labs <- c(
  SP_mean = "SP mean",
  COMP_0_10 = "Composite 0–10 cm",
  COMP_10_20 = "Composite 10–20 cm"
)

site_labs <- c(
  "Midville"     = "Midville: Coastal Plain (North)",
  "Tifton"       = "Tifton: Coastal Plain (South)",
  "Watkinsville" = "Watkinsville: Piedmont",
  "ROME"         = "Rome: Ridge and Valley"
)

plot_vario_site <- function(site_name) {
  df <- empirical_variograms_all %>%
    filter(site == site_name) %>%
    mutate(
      nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = names(nutrient_labs)),
      support = factor(support, levels = c("SP_mean","COMP_0_10","COMP_10_20"))
    )
  
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = dist, y = gamma, color = support)) +
    geom_point(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.8) +
    geom_line(alpha = 0.8) +
    facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free_y", labeller = as_labeller(nutrient_labs)) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(
        "SP_mean" = "#0072B2",
        "COMP_0_10" = "#009E73",
        "COMP_10_20" = "#D55E00"
      ),
      labels = support_labs
    ) +
    labs(
      x = "Distance (m)",
      y = "Semivariance",
      color = "Support",
      title = paste("Empirical Semivariograms -", site_labs[[site_name]])
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
  
  print(p)
  
  ggsave(
    paste0("../output_do_not_push/standard_checks/fig_semivariograms_", site_name, ".png"),
    p, width = 12, height = 8.5, dpi = 300
  )
}

purrr::walk(sites, plot_vario_site)

#P is significant for 3/4 sites, but not Watkinsville. Mn strong, pH strong. rest are mixed. 
#site level- rome is most structured. Tifton is selective. Midville mixed. Watkinsville is least structured.

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Spatial setup
# ---------------------------------------------------------
spatial_dat <- lime_wide %>%
  select(
    site, sample_site, location_id, lat, long,
    ends_with("_SP_mean"),
    ends_with("_COMP_0_10"),
    ends_with("_COMP_10_20")
  )

spatial_sf <- st_as_sf(
  spatial_dat,
  coords = c("long", "lat"),
  crs = 4326
)

spatial_sf_utm <- st_transform(spatial_sf, 32617)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Variable list
# ---------------------------------------------------------
nutrients_all <- c("ca","k","lb_ceq","lbc_1","mg","mn","p","p_h_2","zn")
supports_all  <- c("SP_mean","COMP_0_10","COMP_10_20")
sites <- c("ROME", "Watkinsville", "Midville", "Tifton")

vars_all <- expand.grid(
  nutrient = nutrients_all,
  support = supports_all,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  mutate(variable = paste0(nutrient, "_", support)) %>%
  pull(variable)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Function to fit one variogram
# ---------------------------------------------------------
fit_one_variogram <- function(data_sf, value_col, site_name, model_types = c("Sph", "Exp", "Gau")) {
  
  dat_site <- data_sf %>%
    filter(site == site_name) %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[value_col]]))
  
  dat_sp <- as(dat_site, "Spatial")
  coords <- coordinates(dat_sp)
  
  # empirical variogram
  dmax <- max(dist(coords))
  cutoff <- dmax / 2
  width <- cutoff / 10
  
  form <- as.formula(paste(value_col, "~ 1"))
  v_emp <- gstat::variogram(form, data = dat_sp, cutoff = cutoff, width = width)
  
  # basic starting values
  nug_start  <- min(v_emp$gamma, na.rm = TRUE)
  sill_start <- max(v_emp$gamma, na.rm = TRUE)
  psill_start <- max(sill_start - nug_start, 1e-6)
  range_start <- median(v_emp$dist[v_emp$dist > 0], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # fit candidate models
  fits <- purrr::map(model_types, function(m) {
    tryCatch({
      start_model <- vgm(
        psill = psill_start,
        model = m,
        range = range_start,
        nugget = nug_start
      )
      
      fit <- fit.variogram(v_emp, start_model)
      
      nugget_val <- fit$psill[fit$model == "Nug"]
      if (length(nugget_val) == 0) nugget_val <- 0
      
      struct_rows <- fit$model != "Nug"
      psill_val <- sum(fit$psill[struct_rows], na.rm = TRUE)
      range_val <- max(fit$range[struct_rows], na.rm = TRUE)
      total_sill <- nugget_val + psill_val
      
      tibble(
        site = site_name,
        variable = value_col,
        model = m,
        nugget = nugget_val,
        psill = psill_val,
        sill_total = total_sill,
        range_m = range_val,
        nugget_sill_ratio = ifelse(total_sill > 0, nugget_val / total_sill, NA_real_),
        SSErr = attr(fit, "SSErr"),
        singular = attr(fit, "singular"),
        converged = !isTRUE(attr(fit, "singular"))
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      tibble(
        site = site_name,
        variable = value_col,
        model = m,
        nugget = NA_real_,
        psill = NA_real_,
        sill_total = NA_real_,
        range_m = NA_real_,
        nugget_sill_ratio = NA_real_,
        SSErr = NA_real_,
        singular = NA,
        converged = FALSE
      )
    })
  })
  
  fits_df <- bind_rows(fits)
  
  # keep best fit by lowest SSErr
  best_fit <- fits_df %>%
    arrange(SSErr) %>%
    slice(1) %>%
    mutate(
      nutrient = str_remove(variable, "_(SP_mean|COMP_0_10|COMP_10_20)$"),
      support = str_extract(variable, "(SP_mean|COMP_0_10|COMP_10_20)$"),
      spatial_dependence = case_when(
        is.na(nugget_sill_ratio) ~ "fit failed",
        nugget_sill_ratio < 0.25 ~ "strong",
        nugget_sill_ratio < 0.75 ~ "moderate",
        TRUE ~ "weak"
      )
    ) %>%
    select(
      site, nutrient, support, variable, model,
      nugget, psill, sill_total, range_m,
      nugget_sill_ratio, spatial_dependence,
      SSErr, singular, converged
    )
  
  best_fit
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Run all fits
# ---------------------------------------------------------
variogram_fits_all <- purrr::map_dfr(
  vars_all,
  ~ purrr::map_dfr(sites, \(s) fit_one_variogram(spatial_sf_utm, .x, s))
)

variogram_fits_all

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Save
# ---------------------------------------------------------
write_csv(
  variogram_fits_all,
  "../output_do_not_push/standard_checks/variogram_fits_all.csv"
)
#semivariograms
#Strongest by site: Tifton > Midville > Watkinsville > Rome
#By nutrient: P = pH > Mn = Zn = LBCeq > LBC > K > Mg > Ca
#By sampling: SP_mean > COMP_0_10 > COMP_10_20


#Exploratory spatial analyses showed that spatial dependence differed by nutrient, support, and site. Across all 108 site × nutrient × support combinations, phosphorus exhibited the strongest spatial autocorrelation by Moran’s I (mean = 0.239, median = 0.286, 9 significant results), followed by manganese (mean = 0.181), zinc (mean = 0.180), and pH (mean = 0.175). Rome was the most spatially structured site overall, with mean Moran’s I = 0.294, median = 0.316, and 25 significant site × support combinations, whereas Midville, Watkinsville, and Tifton had mean Moran’s I values of 0.106, 0.097, and 0.096, respectively. Fitted semivariograms further indicated substantial spatial dependence across the dataset: 102 of 108 models converged, and the fitted variograms had an overall mean nugget:sill ratio = 0.215 (median = 0.170). Phosphorus (mean nugget:sill ratio = 0.158, median fitted range = 201.5 m) and pH (mean nugget:sill ratio = 0.132, median fitted range = 231.9 m) were the most consistently spatial properties in the fitted models. Site-specific fitted metrics also varied, with median nugget:sill ratios of 0.374 in Rome, 0.170 in Watkinsville, 0.078 in Midville, and 0.035 in Tifton. Because the main sampling-support comparisons were conducted as repeated within-location tests, these spatial patterns were interpreted as contextual characteristics of the sampled fields rather than as violations requiring a different primary analytical approach.
```

```{r grid_spacing_by_site, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# one row per location already in lime_wide
grid_pts <- lime_wide %>%
  select(site, sample_site, location_id, lat, long) %>%
  distinct()

# convert to sf and project to UTM 17N for distance in meters
grid_sf <- st_as_sf(grid_pts, coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4326) %>%
  st_transform(32617)

coords <- st_coordinates(grid_sf)

grid_sf$x <- coords[,1]
grid_sf$y <- coords[,2]

# nearest-neighbor spacing by site
grid_spacing_summary <- grid_sf %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(site) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    xy <- as.matrix(.x[, c("x", "y")])
    
    # k = 2 because first neighbor is the point itself
    nn <- FNN::get.knn(xy, k = 2)
    nn1 <- nn$nn.dist[, 1]
    nn2 <- nn$nn.dist[, 2]
    
    tibble(
      n_locations = nrow(.x),
      nn1_mean_m = mean(nn1),
      nn1_median_m = median(nn1),
      nn1_min_m = min(nn1),
      nn1_max_m = max(nn1),
      nn2_mean_m = mean(nn2),
      nn2_median_m = median(nn2)
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup()

grid_spacing_summary
library(FNN)

site_labs <- c(
  "Midville"     = "Midville: Coastal Plain (North)",
  "Tifton"       = "Tifton: Coastal Plain (South)",
  "Watkinsville" = "Watkinsville: Piedmont",
  "ROME"         = "Rome: Ridge and Valley"
)

grid_pts <- lime_wide %>%
  select(site, sample_site, location_id, lat, long) %>%
  distinct()

grid_sf <- st_as_sf(grid_pts, coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4326) %>%
  st_transform(32617)

coords <- st_coordinates(grid_sf)
grid_sf$x <- coords[,1]
grid_sf$y <- coords[,2]

nn_plot_df <- grid_sf %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(site) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    xy <- as.matrix(.x[, c("x", "y")])
    nn <- FNN::get.knn(xy, k = 2)
    tibble(nn1_m = nn$nn.dist[,1])
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(site = factor(site, levels = c("ROME", "Watkinsville", "Midville", "Tifton")))

p_spacing <- ggplot(nn_plot_df, aes(x = site, y = nn1_m, fill = site)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.72, outlier.alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = site_labs) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Midville" = "#0072B2",
    "Tifton" = "#009E73",
    "Watkinsville" = "#CC79A7",
    "ROME" = "#D55E00"
  )) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Nearest-neighbor distance (m)",
    title = "Approximate Grid Spacing by Site",
    subtitle = "Based on nearest-neighbor distances among sampling locations"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p_spacing
#Because nearest-neighbor grid spacing was broadly similar among sites (median spacing approximately 57–62 m), differences in observed spatial structure are unlikely to be driven solely by sampling interval and more likely reflect site-specific soil and landscape variation.7
```


```{r fig_main_boxplots_letters, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}


# nicer nutrient labels
nutrient_labs <- c(
  ca     = "Calcium (Ca)",
  k      = "Potassium (K)",
  lb_ceq = "Lime Buffer Capacity (Equivalent)",
  lbc_1  = "Lime Buffer Capacity",
  mg     = "Magnesium (Mg)",
  mn     = "Manganese (Mn)",
  p      = "Phosphorus (P)",
  p_h_2  = "pH",
  zn     = "Zinc (Zn)"
)

support_labs <- c(
  SP_mean    = "Single Point Mean 0–10 cm",
  COMP_0_10  = "Composite 0–10 cm",
  COMP_10_20 = "Composite 10–20 cm"
)

# Okabe-Ito colorblind-friendly palette
support_cols <- c(
  SP_mean    = "#0072B2",
  COMP_0_10  = "#009E73",
  COMP_10_20 = "#D55E00"
)

make_letters <- function(df) {
  comps <- df %>%
    transmute(
      comparison = paste(group1, group2, sep = "-"),
      p.adj = p.adj
    )
  
  pvec <- comps$p.adj
  names(pvec) <- comps$comparison
  
  letters <- multcompView::multcompLetters(pvec, threshold = 0.05)$Letters
  
  tibble(
    sample_support = names(letters),
    letters = letters
  )
}

letters_df <- pairwise_all %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  group_modify(~ make_letters(.x)) %>%
  ungroup()

letter_pos <- repeated_long %>%
  group_by(nutrient, sample_support) %>%
  summarise(
    y_pos = max(value, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.08,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

letters_plot_df <- letters_df %>%
  left_join(letter_pos, by = c("nutrient", "sample_support")) %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = names(nutrient_labs)),
    sample_support = factor(sample_support, levels = c("SP_mean", "COMP_0_10", "COMP_10_20"))
  )

plot_df <- repeated_long %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = names(nutrient_labs)),
    sample_support = factor(sample_support, levels = c("SP_mean", "COMP_0_10", "COMP_10_20"))
  )

p_main <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = sample_support, y = value, fill = sample_support)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.72,
    outlier.alpha = 0.35,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = letters_plot_df,
    aes(x = sample_support, y = y_pos, label = letters),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ nutrient,
    scales = "free_y",
    labeller = as_labeller(nutrient_labs)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = support_cols) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = support_labs) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Observed value",
    title = "Soil-Property Distributions by Sampling Support Across All Bermuda Hay Grassland Sites",
    subtitle = "Different letters indicate significant pairwise differences within each nutrient (paired Wilcoxon, BH-adjusted)"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 30, l = 20)
  )

p_main

ggsave(
  "../output_do_not_push/fig_main_boxplots_letters.png",
  p_main,
  width = 12.5,
  height = 8.75,
  dpi = 300
)

```

```{r fig_absdiff_by_site_letters, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}


# nutrient labels
nutrient_labs <- c(
  ca     = "Calcium (Ca)",
  k      = "Potassium (K)",
  lb_ceq = "Lime Buffer Capacity (Equivalent)",
  lbc_1  = "Lime Buffer Capacity",
  mg     = "Magnesium (Mg)",
  mn     = "Manganese (Mn)",
  p      = "Phosphorus (P)",
  p_h_2  = "pH",
  zn     = "Zinc (Zn)"
)

site_labs <- c(
  "Midville"     = "Midville: Coastal Plain (North)",
  "Tifton"       = "Tifton: Coastal Plain (South)",
  "Watkinsville" = "Watkinsville: Piedmont",
  "ROME"         = "Rome: Ridge and Valley"
)

site_order <- c("ROME", "Watkinsville", "Midville", "Tifton")

ab_plot_df_site <- ab_variability %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = names(nutrient_labs)),
    site = factor(site, levels = site_order)
  )

# Kruskal-Wallis by nutrient
kw_absdiff <- ab_plot_df_site %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  kruskal_test(abs_diff ~ site) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(p_adj_BH = p.adjust(p, method = "BH"))

# Dunn post hoc by nutrient
dunn_absdiff <- ab_plot_df_site %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  dunn_test(abs_diff ~ site, p.adjust.method = "BH") %>%
  ungroup()

# compact letters
make_site_letters <- function(df) {
  comps <- df %>%
    transmute(
      comparison = paste(group1, group2, sep = "-"),
      p.adj = p.adj
    )
  
  pvec <- comps$p.adj
  names(pvec) <- comps$comparison
  
  letters <- multcompView::multcompLetters(pvec, threshold = 0.05)$Letters
  
  tibble(
    site = names(letters),
    letters = letters
  )
}

letters_site_df <- dunn_absdiff %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  group_modify(~ make_site_letters(.x)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = names(nutrient_labs)),
    site = factor(site, levels = site_order)
  )

letter_pos_site <- ab_plot_df_site %>%
  group_by(nutrient, site) %>%
  summarise(
    y_pos = max(abs_diff, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.08 * diff(range(abs_diff, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

letters_plot_site <- letters_site_df %>%
  left_join(letter_pos_site, by = c("nutrient", "site"))

p_ab_site <- ggplot(ab_plot_df_site, aes(x = site, y = abs_diff, fill = site)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    width = 0.72,
    outlier.alpha = 0.35,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = letters_plot_site,
    aes(x = site, y = y_pos, label = letters),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 4.4,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ nutrient,
    scales = "free_y",
    labeller = as_labeller(nutrient_labs)
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = site_labs) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Midville" = "#0072B2",
      "Tifton" = "#009E73",
      "Watkinsville" = "#CC79A7",
      "ROME" = "#D55E00"
    ),
    labels = site_labs
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "|SP A - SP B|",
    title = "Local-Scale Variability Between Single-Point Samples 10 m Apart",
    subtitle = "Different letters indicate significant site differences in |SP A - SP B| within each nutrient"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 30, l = 20)
  )

p_ab_site

ggsave(
  "../output_do_not_push/fig_SPAB_absdiff_by_site_letters.png",
  p_ab_site,
  width = 12.5,
  height = 8.75,
  dpi = 300
)
```


```{r fig_spa_spb_cleaner, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}


# nutrient labels
nutrient_labs <- c(
  ca     = "Calcium (Ca)",
  k      = "Potassium (K)",
  lb_ceq = "Lime Buffer Capacity (Equivalent)",
  lbc_1  = "Lime Buffer Capacity",
  mg     = "Magnesium (Mg)",
  mn     = "Manganese (Mn)",
  p      = "Phosphorus (P)",
  p_h_2  = "pH",
  zn     = "Zinc (Zn)"
)

# site labels
site_labs <- c(
  "Midville"     = "Midville\nCoastal Plain (North)",
  "Tifton"       = "Tifton\nCoastal Plain (South)",
  "Watkinsville" = "Watkinsville\nPiedmont",
  "ROME"         = "Rome\nRidge and Valley"
)

site_order <- c("ROME", "Watkinsville", "Midville", "Tifton")

sp_labs <- c(
  "SP_A" = "A",
  "SP_B" = "B"
)

sp_cols <- c(
  "SP_A" = "#0072B2",
  "SP_B" = "#D55E00"
)

# long table for SP A vs SP B
ab_long_for_plot <- lime_tagged %>%
  filter(sample_type %in% c("SP_A", "SP_B")) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(nutrients_all), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = names(nutrient_labs)),
    site = factor(site, levels = site_order),
    sample_type = factor(sample_type, levels = c("SP_A", "SP_B"))
  )

# significance labels from existing paired Wilcoxon by site
sig_sp_by_site <- ab_wilcox_by_site %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = names(nutrient_labs)),
    site = factor(site, levels = site_order),
    sig = case_when(
      p.adj <= 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.adj <= 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.adj <= 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ "ns"
    )
  )

# y-position for significance labels within each nutrient x site
sig_pos_sp <- ab_long_for_plot %>%
  group_by(nutrient, site) %>%
  summarise(
    y_pos = max(value, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.08 * diff(range(value, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

sig_plot_sp <- sig_sp_by_site %>%
  left_join(sig_pos_sp, by = c("nutrient", "site"))

p_spab_clean <- ggplot(ab_long_for_plot, aes(x = site, y = value, fill = sample_type)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.78),
    width = 0.68,
    outlier.alpha = 0.35,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = sig_plot_sp,
    aes(x = site, y = y_pos, label = sig),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 4.4,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ nutrient,
    scales = "free_y",
    labeller = as_labeller(nutrient_labs)
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = site_labs) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = sp_cols,
    labels = sp_labs
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Observed value",
    fill = "Single-point sample",
    title = "Differences Between Single-Point Samples Collected 10 m Apart",
    subtitle = "Within each site, SP A and SP B are compared using paired Wilcoxon tests"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 20, l = 20)
  )

p_spab_clean

ggsave(
  "../output_do_not_push/fig_SPAB_cleaner_by_site.png",
  p_spab_clean,
  width = 20,
  height = 12,
  dpi = 300
)

```

```{r support_variability_summary, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)

support_variability <- repeated_long %>%
  group_by(site, nutrient, sample_support) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    median = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = IQR(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    cv_pct = ifelse(mean > 0, 100 * sd / mean, NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

support_variability

write_csv(
  support_variability,
  "../output_do_not_push/support_variability_summary.csv"
)



```

```{r support_variability_figure, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
nutrient_labs <- c(
  ca     = "Calcium (Ca)",
  k      = "Potassium (K)",
  lb_ceq = "Lime Buffer Capacity (Equivalent)",
  lbc_1  = "Lime Buffer Capacity",
  mg     = "Magnesium (Mg)",
  mn     = "Manganese (Mn)",
  p      = "Phosphorus (P)",
  p_h_2  = "pH",
  zn     = "Zinc (Zn)"
)

support_labs <- c(
  SP_mean    = "SP Mean 0–10 cm",
  COMP_0_10  = "Composite 0–10 cm",
  COMP_10_20 = "Composite 10–20 cm"
)

support_cols <- c(
  SP_mean    = "#0072B2",
  COMP_0_10  = "#009E73",
  COMP_10_20 = "#D55E00"
)

p_cv <- support_variability %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = names(nutrient_labs)),
    sample_support = factor(sample_support, levels = c("SP_mean","COMP_0_10","COMP_10_20"))
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = sample_support, y = cv_pct, fill = sample_support)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_grid(nutrient ~ site, scales = "free_y", labeller = labeller(nutrient = as_labeller(nutrient_labs))) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = support_labs) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = support_cols) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Coefficient of variation (%)",
    title = "Support-level variability by nutrient and site"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p_cv

ggsave(
  "../output_do_not_push/fig_support_variability_cv.png",
  p_cv,
  width = 13,
  height = 10,
  dpi = 300
)
#to show how support changes spread.
```



```{r effect_size_data, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

# build location-level percent differences
effect_size_long <- purrr::map_dfr(nutrients_all, function(nut) {
  sp <- lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_SP_mean")]]
  c01 <- lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_COMP_0_10")]]
  c1020 <- lime_wide[[paste0(nut, "_COMP_10_20")]]
  
  tibble(
    site = lime_wide$site,
    sample_site = lime_wide$sample_site,
    location_id = lime_wide$location_id,
    nutrient = nut,
    pct_diff_C0_10_vs_SP = 100 * (c01 - sp) / sp,
    pct_diff_C10_20_vs_SP = 100 * (c1020 - sp) / sp
  )
}) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(pct_diff_C0_10_vs_SP, pct_diff_C10_20_vs_SP),
    names_to = "contrast",
    values_to = "pct_diff"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    contrast = case_when(
      contrast == "pct_diff_C0_10_vs_SP"  ~ "Composite 0–10 vs SP Mean",
      contrast == "pct_diff_C10_20_vs_SP" ~ "Composite 10–20 vs SP Mean",
      TRUE ~ contrast
    )
  )

effect_size_summary <- effect_size_long %>%
  group_by(site, nutrient, contrast) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median_pct_diff = median(pct_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_pct_diff = mean(pct_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_pct_diff = IQR(pct_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

effect_size_summary

write_csv(
  effect_size_summary,
  "../output_do_not_push/effect_size_summary.csv"
)
```


```{r effect_size_figure, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

nutrient_labs <- c(
  ca     = "Calcium (Ca)",
  k      = "Potassium (K)",
  lb_ceq = "Lime Buffer Capacity (Equivalent)",
  lbc_1  = "Lime Buffer Capacity",
  mg     = "Magnesium (Mg)",
  mn     = "Manganese (Mn)",
  p      = "Phosphorus (P)",
  p_h_2  = "pH",
  zn     = "Zinc (Zn)"
)

contrast_cols <- c(
  "Composite 0–10 vs SP Mean" = "#009E73",
  "Composite 10–20 vs SP Mean" = "#D55E00"
)

# pooled effect-size summary
effect_size_pooled <- effect_size_long %>%
  group_by(nutrient, contrast) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median_pct_diff = median(pct_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    q1 = quantile(pct_diff, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3 = quantile(pct_diff, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_pct_diff = IQR(pct_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = names(nutrient_labs)),
    contrast = factor(
      contrast,
      levels = c("Composite 0–10 vs SP Mean", "Composite 10–20 vs SP Mean")
    )
  )

# force each facet to be symmetric around zero
facet_limits <- effect_size_pooled %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  summarise(
    lim = max(abs(c(q1, q3, median_pct_diff)), na.rm = TRUE) * 1.15,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

blank_df <- facet_limits %>%
  tidyr::expand_grid(
    contrast = factor(
      c("Composite 0–10 vs SP Mean", "Composite 10–20 vs SP Mean"),
      levels = c("Composite 0–10 vs SP Mean", "Composite 10–20 vs SP Mean")
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    y_upper = lim,
    y_lower = -lim
  )

p_effect_pooled <- ggplot(
  effect_size_pooled,
  aes(x = contrast, y = median_pct_diff, fill = contrast)
) +
  geom_blank(
    data = blank_df,
    aes(x = contrast, y = y_upper),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_blank(
    data = blank_df,
    aes(x = contrast, y = y_lower),
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_col(width = 0.72) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = q1, ymax = q3),
    width = 0.18,
    linewidth = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(
    ~ nutrient,
    scales = "free_y",
    labeller = as_labeller(nutrient_labs)
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = contrast_cols) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Median % difference from single-point mean\n(error bars = IQR)",
    fill = "Sampling Comparison",
    title = "Magnitude and direction of composite sampling effects relative to single-point mean"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

p_effect_pooled

ggsave(
  "../output_do_not_push/fig_effect_size_percent_diff_pooled.png",
  p_effect_pooled,
  width = 10.5,
  height = 7.5,
  dpi = 300
)
#P and Zn are highly sensitive to the deeper composite support

#Composite 0–10 cm is usually slightly to moderately higher than SP mean

# Composite 10–20 cm is usually lower than SP mean
# #pH behaves differently from the extractable nutrients, where Composite 10–20 cm is often higher than SP mean

#recommendations:
# If the goal is to represent near-surface fertility status for routine agronomic interpretation in these grassland systems, Composite 0–10 cm is recommended. In the data, it generally preserved the same directional story as SP while giving slightly higher near-surface values for most nutrients, and it avoids the very large downward shifts seen with the 10–20 cm composite for P and Zn. However, for pH and K, either SP or COMP is interchangeable, as there were overall general non-significant differences in sampling of the upper 10cm. This was reflected across most sites. 
#If the goal is to capture local, fine-scale heterogeneity, SP is more informative. The A–B results show that single points 10 m apart can differ meaningfully; SP is better at revealing patchiness rather than smoothing it away.
```




```{r site_scaled_heatmap, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)

nutrient_labs <- c(
  ca     = "Calcium (Ca)",
  k      = "Potassium (K)",
  lb_ceq = "Lime Buffer Capacity (Equivalent)",
  lbc_1  = "Lime Buffer Capacity",
  mg     = "Magnesium (Mg)",
  mn     = "Manganese (Mn)",
  p      = "Phosphorus (P)",
  p_h_2  = "pH",
  zn     = "Zinc (Zn)"
)

site_labs <- c(
  "ROME" = "Rome",
  "Watkinsville" = "Watkinsville",
  "Midville" = "Midville",
  "Tifton" = "Tifton"
)

heat_df <- effect_size_summary %>%
  group_by(nutrient, contrast) %>%
  mutate(
    scaled_effect = if (sd(median_pct_diff, na.rm = TRUE) == 0) {
      0
    } else {
      as.numeric(scale(median_pct_diff))
    }
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = names(nutrient_labs)),
    site = factor(site, levels = c("ROME", "Watkinsville", "Midville", "Tifton")),
    label = paste0(round(scaled_effect, 2), "\n(", round(median_pct_diff, 1), "%)")
  )

p_heat_site_scaled <- ggplot(heat_df, aes(x = site, y = nutrient, fill = scaled_effect)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = label), size = 3.0, lineheight = 0.95) +
  facet_wrap(~ contrast) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = site_labs) +
  scale_y_discrete(labels = nutrient_labs) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#D55E00",
    mid = "white",
    high = "#009E73",
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1.5, 1.5),
    oob = scales::squish,
    name = "Scaled effect\nwithin nutrient"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    title = "Site-level support effects relative to SP mean",
    subtitle = "Tile color and top number are scaled within each nutrient and contrast; raw median % difference is shown in parentheses"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

p_heat_site_scaled

ggsave(
  "../output_do_not_push/fig_site_scaled_heatmap.png",
  p_heat_site_scaled,
  width = 11,
  height = 7.5,
  dpi = 300
)
```

```{r PERRY}
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Read data
#    YOU fill in the file paths
# ---------------------------------------------------------
ap_trunc <- read_csv("../data_do_not_push/hz_0_15_wide_03_30_2026.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
ap_full  <- read_csv("../data_do_not_push/perry_Ap_data_full_02_10_2026.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
fixed_15 <- read_csv("../data_do_not_push/perry_soil_points_surface_03_2026.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Keep only fertility variables
#    No C or N
# ---------------------------------------------------------
soil_vars <- c("lbc_1", "lb_ceq", "p_h_2", "ca", "k", "mg", "mn", "p", "zn")

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Standardize names
#    Adjust these renames if your columns differ slightly
# ---------------------------------------------------------

ap_trunc2 <- ap_trunc %>%
  transmute(
    point = Point,
    ca = Ca_0_15,
    k = K_0_15,
    mg = Mg_0_15,
    mn = Mn_0_15,
    p = P_0_15,
    zn = Zn_0_15,
    p_h_2 = `pH.2_0_15`,
    lbc_1 = `LBC.1_0_15`,
    lb_ceq = LBCeq_0_15,
    POINT_X,
    POINT_Y
  ) %>%
  distinct()

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Standardize full Ap
# multiple rows per point, so collapse to one row per point
# I recommend thickness-weighted mean using H.Depth.cm.
# ---------------------------------------------------------
ap_full2 <- ap_full %>%
  transmute(
    point = Point,
    hz_thickness = `H.Depth.cm.`,
    ca = Ca,
    k = K,
    mg = Mg,
    mn = Mn,
    p = P,
    zn = Zn,
    p_h_2 = `pH.2`,
    lbc_1 = `LBC.1`,
    lb_ceq = LBCeq,
    POINT_X,
    POINT_Y
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(point), !is.na(hz_thickness), hz_thickness > 0)

# thickness-weighted one row per point
ap_full_point <- ap_full2 %>%
  group_by(point) %>%
  summarise(
    ca = weighted.mean(ca, hz_thickness, na.rm = TRUE),
    k = weighted.mean(k, hz_thickness, na.rm = TRUE),
    mg = weighted.mean(mg, hz_thickness, na.rm = TRUE),
    mn = weighted.mean(mn, hz_thickness, na.rm = TRUE),
    p = weighted.mean(p, hz_thickness, na.rm = TRUE),
    zn = weighted.mean(zn, hz_thickness, na.rm = TRUE),
    p_h_2 = weighted.mean(p_h_2, hz_thickness, na.rm = TRUE),
    lbc_1 = weighted.mean(lbc_1, hz_thickness, na.rm = TRUE),
    lb_ceq = weighted.mean(lb_ceq, hz_thickness, na.rm = TRUE),
    POINT_X = first(POINT_X),
    POINT_Y = first(POINT_Y),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Standardize fixed depth
# choose CID as main fertility comparison
# ---------------------------------------------------------
fixed_152 <- fixed_15 %>%
  filter(type == "CID") %>%
  transmute(
    point = PointLabel,
    ca = Ca,
    k = K,
    mg = Mg,
    mn = Mn,
    p = P,
    zn = Zn,
    p_h_2 = `pH.2`,
    lbc_1 = `LBC.1`,
    lb_ceq = LBCeq,
    x,
    y
  ) %>%
  distinct()

# quick checks
names(ap_trunc2)
names(ap_full_point)
names(fixed_152)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Summary stats for each dataset
# ---------------------------------------------------------
summarize_dataset <- function(df, dataset_name) {
  df %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = all_of(soil_vars), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value") %>%
    group_by(nutrient) %>%
    summarise(
      dataset = dataset_name,
      n = sum(!is.na(value)),
      mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
      median = median(value, na.rm = TRUE),
      sd = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
      IQR = IQR(value, na.rm = TRUE),
      cv_pct = ifelse(mean > 0, 100 * sd / mean, NA_real_),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

summary_fixed <- summarize_dataset(fixed_152, "Fixed_0_15_CID")
summary_ap_trunc <- summarize_dataset(ap_trunc2, "Ap_truncated_0_15")
summary_ap_full <- summarize_dataset(ap_full_point, "Ap_full")

summary_all <- bind_rows(summary_fixed, summary_ap_trunc, summary_ap_full)
summary_all

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 6. PAIRED comparison: truncated Ap vs full Ap
# same points
# ---------------------------------------------------------
ap_paired <- ap_trunc2 %>%
  inner_join(ap_full_point, by = "point", suffix = c("_trunc", "_full"))

paired_tests <- purrr::map_dfr(soil_vars, function(v) {
  x <- ap_paired[[paste0(v, "_trunc")]]
  y <- ap_paired[[paste0(v, "_full")]]
  
  tmp <- tibble(x = x, y = y) %>% drop_na()
  
  wt <- wilcox.test(tmp$x, tmp$y, paired = TRUE, exact = FALSE)
  ct <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(tmp$x, tmp$y, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
  
  tibble(
    nutrient = v,
    n = nrow(tmp),
    median_trunc = median(tmp$x, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_full = median(tmp$y, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_diff = median(tmp$y - tmp$x, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_pct_diff = median(ifelse(tmp$x == 0, NA, 100 * (tmp$y - tmp$x) / tmp$x), na.rm = TRUE),
    wilcox_p = wt$p.value,
    spearman_rho = unname(ct$estimate),
    spearman_p = ct$p.value
  )
}) %>%
  mutate(
    wilcox_p_adj_BH = p.adjust(wilcox_p, method = "BH"),
    spearman_p_adj_BH = p.adjust(spearman_p, method = "BH")
  )
paired_tests

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 7. UNPAIRED comparison: fixed depth vs truncated Ap
# ---------------------------------------------------------
fixed_vs_trunc <- purrr::map_dfr(soil_vars, function(v) {
  x <- fixed_152[[v]]
  y <- ap_trunc2[[v]]
  
  tmp <- tibble(
    group = c(rep("Fixed_0_15_CID", length(x)), rep("Ap_truncated_0_15", length(y))),
    value = c(x, y)
  ) %>% drop_na()
  
  wt <- wilcox.test(value ~ group, data = tmp, exact = FALSE)
  
  tibble(
    nutrient = v,
    n_fixed = sum(!is.na(x)),
    n_ap_trunc = sum(!is.na(y)),
    median_fixed = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_ap_trunc = median(y, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_diff = median(y, na.rm = TRUE) - median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_pct_diff = 100 * (median(y, na.rm = TRUE) - median(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    wilcox_p = wt$p.value
  )
}) %>%
  mutate(wilcox_p_adj_BH = p.adjust(wilcox_p, method = "BH"))
fixed_vs_trunc

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 8. UNPAIRED comparison: fixed depth vs full Ap
# ---------------------------------------------------------
fixed_vs_full <- purrr::map_dfr(soil_vars, function(v) {
  x <- fixed_152[[v]]
  y <- ap_full_point[[v]]
  
  tmp <- tibble(
    group = c(rep("Fixed_0_15_CID", length(x)), rep("Ap_full", length(y))),
    value = c(x, y)
  ) %>% drop_na()
  
  wt <- wilcox.test(value ~ group, data = tmp, exact = FALSE)
  
  tibble(
    nutrient = v,
    n_fixed = sum(!is.na(x)),
    n_ap_full = sum(!is.na(y)),
    median_fixed = median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_ap_full = median(y, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_diff = median(y, na.rm = TRUE) - median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_pct_diff = 100 * (median(y, na.rm = TRUE) - median(x, na.rm = TRUE)) / median(x, na.rm = TRUE),
    wilcox_p = wt$p.value
  )
}) %>%
  mutate(wilcox_p_adj_BH = p.adjust(wilcox_p, method = "BH"))
fixed_vs_full

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 9. Variability comparison table
# ---------------------------------------------------------
variability_compare <- summary_all %>%
  select(dataset, nutrient, n, median, IQR, sd, cv_pct) %>%
  arrange(nutrient, dataset)

variability_compare

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 10. Figures
# ---------------------------------------------------------
nutrient_labs <- c(
  ca = "Calcium (Ca)",
  k = "Potassium (K)",
  lb_ceq = "LBCeq",
  lbc_1 = "LBC",
  mg = "Magnesium (Mg)",
  mn = "Manganese (Mn)",
  p = "Phosphorus (P)",
  p_h_2 = "pH",
  zn = "Zinc (Zn)"
)
fixed_152 <- fixed_152 %>%
  mutate(point = as.character(point))

ap_trunc2 <- ap_trunc2 %>%
  mutate(point = as.character(point))

ap_full_point <- ap_full_point %>%
  mutate(point = as.character(point))
plot_long <- bind_rows(
  fixed_152 %>% mutate(dataset = "Fixed_0_15_CID"),
  ap_trunc2 %>% mutate(dataset = "Ap_truncated_0_15"),
  ap_full_point %>% mutate(dataset = "Ap_full")
) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(soil_vars), names_to = "nutrient", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = names(nutrient_labs)),
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("Fixed_0_15_CID", "Ap_truncated_0_15", "Ap_full"))
  )

p_box <- ggplot(plot_long, aes(x = dataset, y = value, fill = dataset)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.35, width = 0.72) +
  facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free_y", labeller = as_labeller(nutrient_labs)) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Observed value",
    title = "Fixed-depth and Ap-based soil fertility comparisons"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

p_box

paired_scatter <- purrr::map_dfr(soil_vars, function(v) {
  tibble(
    nutrient = v,
    trunc = ap_paired[[paste0(v, "_trunc")]],
    full = ap_paired[[paste0(v, "_full")]]
  )
}) %>%
  mutate(nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = names(nutrient_labs)))

p_scatter <- ggplot(paired_scatter, aes(x = trunc, y = full)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free", labeller = as_labeller(nutrient_labs)) +
  labs(
    x = "Truncated Ap (0-15 cm)",
    y = "Full Ap",
    title = "Agreement between truncated and full Ap-based fertility values"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

p_scatter

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 11. Save outputs
# ---------------------------------------------------------
write_csv(summary_all, "../output_do_not_push/ap_vs_depth_summary_all.csv")
write_csv(paired_tests, "../output_do_not_push/ap_vs_depth_paired_trunc_vs_full.csv")
write_csv(fixed_vs_trunc, "../output_do_not_push/ap_vs_depth_fixed_vs_trunc.csv")
write_csv(fixed_vs_full, "../output_do_not_push/ap_vs_depth_fixed_vs_full.csv")
write_csv(variability_compare, "../output_do_not_push/ap_vs_depth_variability_compare.csv")
```

```{r}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Build pairwise p-value table across the three datasets
# ---------------------------------------------------------
pairwise_ap <- bind_rows(
  fixed_vs_trunc %>%
    transmute(
      nutrient,
      group1 = "Fixed_0_15_CID",
      group2 = "Ap_truncated_0_15",
      p_adj = wilcox_p_adj_BH
    ),
  fixed_vs_full %>%
    transmute(
      nutrient,
      group1 = "Fixed_0_15_CID",
      group2 = "Ap_full",
      p_adj = wilcox_p_adj_BH
    ),
  paired_tests %>%
    transmute(
      nutrient,
      group1 = "Ap_truncated_0_15",
      group2 = "Ap_full",
      p_adj = wilcox_p_adj_BH
    )
)

pairwise_ap

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Convert pairwise p-values into compact letters
# ---------------------------------------------------------
make_letters_ap <- function(df) {
  comps <- df %>%
    transmute(
      comparison = paste(group1, group2, sep = "-"),
      p_adj = p_adj
    )
  
  pvec <- comps$p_adj
  names(pvec) <- comps$comparison
  
  letters <- multcompView::multcompLetters(pvec, threshold = 0.05)$Letters
  
  tibble(
    dataset = names(letters),
    letters = letters
  )
}

letters_ap_df <- pairwise_ap %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  group_modify(~ make_letters_ap(.x)) %>%
  ungroup()

letters_ap_df

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Get y-positions for letter placement
# ---------------------------------------------------------
letter_pos_ap <- plot_long %>%
  group_by(nutrient, dataset) %>%
  summarise(
    y_pos = max(value, na.rm = TRUE) + 0.08 * diff(range(value, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

letters_plot_ap <- letters_ap_df %>%
  left_join(letter_pos_ap, by = c("nutrient", "dataset")) %>%
  mutate(
    nutrient = factor(nutrient, levels = names(nutrient_labs)),
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = c("Fixed_0_15_CID", "Ap_truncated_0_15", "Ap_full"))
  )

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Make labelled boxplot
# ---------------------------------------------------------
dataset_labs <- c(
  "Fixed_0_15_CID" = "Fixed depth\n0–15 cm",
  "Ap_truncated_0_15" = "Ap truncated\n0–15 cm",
  "Ap_full" = "Full Ap"
)

dataset_cols <- c(
  "Fixed_0_15_CID" = "#0072B2",
  "Ap_truncated_0_15" = "#009E73",
  "Ap_full" = "#D55E00"
)

p_box_letters <- ggplot(plot_long, aes(x = dataset, y = value, fill = dataset)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.35, width = 0.72) +
  geom_text(
    data = letters_plot_ap,
    aes(x = dataset, y = y_pos, label = letters),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free_y", labeller = as_labeller(nutrient_labs)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = dataset_labs) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = dataset_cols) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Observed value",
    title = "Fixed-depth and Ap-based soil fertility comparisons",
    subtitle = "Different letters indicate significant pairwise differences within each nutrient"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 25, l = 15)
  )

p_box_letters

ggsave(
  "../output_do_not_push/ap_vs_depth_boxplots_letters.png",
  p_box_letters,
  width = 11,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)
```
```{r}
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Build significance labels
# ---------------------------------------------------------
sig_label <- function(p) {
  case_when(
    is.na(p)      ~ "ns",
    p <= 0.001    ~ "***",
    p <= 0.01     ~ "**",
    p <= 0.05     ~ "*",
    TRUE          ~ "ns"
  )
}

sig_df <- bind_rows(
  fixed_vs_trunc %>%
    transmute(
      nutrient,
      group1 = "Fixed_0_15_CID",
      group2 = "Ap_truncated_0_15",
      test_code = "RS",
      p_adj = wilcox_p_adj_BH
    ),
  fixed_vs_full %>%
    transmute(
      nutrient,
      group1 = "Fixed_0_15_CID",
      group2 = "Ap_full",
      test_code = "RS",
      p_adj = wilcox_p_adj_BH
    ),
  paired_tests %>%
    transmute(
      nutrient,
      group1 = "Ap_truncated_0_15",
      group2 = "Ap_full",
      test_code = "SR",
      p_adj = wilcox_p_adj_BH
    )
    ) %>%
 mutate(sig = sig_label(p_adj),
        label = paste(test_code, sig)) %>%
  filter(sig != "ns")

sig_df

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Set factor order
# ---------------------------------------------------------
dataset_levels <- c("Fixed_0_15_CID", "Ap_truncated_0_15", "Ap_full")

plot_long2 <- plot_long %>%
  mutate(dataset = factor(dataset, levels = dataset_levels))

sig_df <- sig_df %>%
  mutate(
    group1 = factor(group1, levels = dataset_levels),
    group2 = factor(group2, levels = dataset_levels)
  )

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Build y positions for brackets
# ---------------------------------------------------------
panel_ranges <- plot_long2 %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  summarise(
    y_max = max(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    y_min = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    y_span = y_max - y_min,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

sig_plot_df <- sig_df %>%
  left_join(panel_ranges, by = "nutrient") %>%
  group_by(nutrient) %>%
  arrange(group1, group2, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    bracket_level = row_number(),
    y = y_max + bracket_level * 0.08 * ifelse(y_span == 0, 1, y_span),
    x1 = as.numeric(group1),
    x2 = as.numeric(group2),
    x_mid = (x1 + x2) / 2
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

sig_plot_df

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Plot with brackets
# ---------------------------------------------------------
dataset_labs <- c(
  "Fixed_0_15_CID" = "Fixed depth\n0–15 cm",
  "Ap_truncated_0_15" = "Ap truncated\n0–15 cm",
  "Ap_full" = "Full Ap"
)

dataset_cols <- c(
  "Fixed_0_15_CID" = "#0072B2",
  "Ap_truncated_0_15" = "#009E73",
  "Ap_full" = "#D55E00"
)

p_box_brackets <- ggplot(plot_long2, aes(x = dataset, y = value, fill = dataset)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.35, width = 0.72) +
  
  # bracket verticals
  geom_segment(
    data = sig_plot_df,
    aes(x = x1, xend = x1, y = y - 0.02 * y_span, yend = y),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = sig_plot_df,
    aes(x = x2, xend = x2, y = y - 0.02 * y_span, yend = y),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  # bracket top
  geom_segment(
    data = sig_plot_df,
    aes(x = x1, xend = x2, y = y, yend = y),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 0.5
  ) +
  # bracket label
  geom_text(
    data = sig_plot_df,
    aes(x = x_mid, y = y + 0.04 * y_span, label = label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 3.3,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  facet_wrap(~ nutrient, scales = "free_y", labeller = as_labeller(nutrient_labs)) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = dataset_labs) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = dataset_cols) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Observed value",
    title = "Fixed-depth and Ap-based soil fertility comparisons",
    subtitle = "RS = Wilcoxon rank-sum; SR = Wilcoxon signed-rank",
    caption = "* p ≤ 0.05   ** p ≤ 0.01   *** p ≤ 0.001"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
  legend.position = "none",
  axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1),
  strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
  panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
  plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 25, l = 15),
  plot.caption = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 10))

p_box_brackets

ggsave(
  "../output_do_not_push/ap_vs_depth_boxplots_brackets.png",
  p_box_brackets,
  width = 12,
  height = 10,
  dpi = 300
)
```
